title: What is CAS?
subtitle: Creativity, Activity, Service
introduction: CAS (Creativity, Activity, Service) is one of the core components of the IB Diploma Programme. It involves students in a range of activities alongside their academic studies. CAS is structured around the three strands that give it its name.

sections.title: Creativity
sections.description: Exploring and extending ideas leading to an original or interpretive product or performance.

sections.title: Activity
sections.description: Physical exertion contributing to a healthy lifestyle, complementing academic work elsewhere in the Diploma Programme.

sections.title: Service
sections.description: Collaborative and reciprocal engagement with the community in response to an authentic need.

objectives: Enjoy and find significance in their work
Develop the ability to work collaboratively
Engage with issues of global importance
Consider the ethical implications of their actions
Develop greater awareness of their own strengths and areas for growth
